sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports experiencing persistent, sharp pain in his right knee, particularly when bending or squatting. He states the pain started approximately two weeks ago after a minor fall. He denies any fever or chills. The patient also reports some stiffness in the knee, especially in the morning.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals mild tenderness to palpation over the medial aspect of the right knee. Range of motion is limited by pain, with a noticeable decrease in flexion. Lachman's test is negative. McMurray's test is negative.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: Right knee pain, likely a sprain or strain. Differential includes meniscus injury.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe ice packs to the affected area three times daily. Recommend over-the-counter NSAIDs for pain relief. Encourage gentle range-of-motion exercises as tolerated. Schedule follow-up in one week to assess progress.
